title: Substack vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack and Campaign Monitor. See pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
h1: Substack vs Campaign Monitor: Detailed Comparison
intro: Substack and Campaign Monitor serve different email marketing needs. Substack is built for individual writers and creators monetizing newsletters directly, while Campaign Monitor targets agencies and businesses sending professional campaigns. Understanding their core differences will help you choose the right tool for your goals.

comparison_table.tool_b: Agencies and teams managing multiple brands

comparison_table.tool_a: Paid subscriptions, podcast hosting, notes feed, built-in audience
comparison_table.tool_b: Template builder, journey designer, multi-brand management, transactional email

choose_a_if: You're an individual creator or writer wanting to monetize newsletters directly without upfront costs
You need unlimited subscriber capacity and don't require advanced segmentation or multi-brand features
You want an integrated audience discovery and notes feed in addition to email functionality
choose_b_if: You're an agency or business managing multiple brands and need centralized campaign management
You require advanced automation features like journey designer and smart segments for customer workflows
You need transactional email capabilities and professional analytics beyond basic email sending

faq.q: Is Substack better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Neither is universally better—they serve different use cases. Substack excels for individual creators monetizing content directly, while Campaign Monitor is superior for businesses and agencies needing professional email marketing with advanced automation. Your choice depends on your primary goal: content monetization or customer communication.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Substack is cheaper for most users since it's completely free with no subscriber limits. You only pay Substack a percentage of revenue if you offer paid subscriptions. Campaign Monitor's free tier doesn't exist; plans start at $11/month, making it more expensive upfront but offering more enterprise-grade features.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your subscriber lists from Campaign Monitor and import them into Substack. However, the tools have fundamentally different architectures—Substack focuses on monetization and audience building, while Campaign Monitor is built for marketing campaigns. The transition works best if you're shifting your strategy rather than simply moving platforms.
